patterns_recognized: F1*-starred GOLD picks without F3 ≥ 1000 as an independent corroborator produced three consecutive losses across two slates (MIL G1/G2 7/11, SEA@TB 7/12): F1* and SP-xERA draw from the same thin Statcast pool and are partially redundant, not independent corroborators. F3_swing_take_gap ≥ 1000 is now required as a third anchor before confirming any starred-F1 pick at GOLD or above.

Stage 2 full-game direction has consistently outperformed Stage 1 in high f5_full_delta zones (≥ 0.15) across 7/10-7/12: three consecutive large-delta cases (LAA@MIN delta=0.224, SEA@TB delta=0.242, NYY@WSH delta=0.293) all resolved in Stage 2's direction. Do not treat Stage 1 lean as an OVERRIDE trigger against Stage 2.

D-grade dual-opposing-signal picks (SP xERA against + PQI against) split 4W/3L directionally on 7/11 — directional ambiguity is genuine. The only correct response is SKIP; OVERRIDE toward the opposing team is not warranted even when both signals simultaneously oppose the model pick.

CAP 10 bottom-bucket CONFIRM-rescued picks hit 47.6% vs 31.2% non-CONFIRM (archive through 5/18), but the rescued rate is itself below 50% breakeven. The four-dimension rescue rule (F-signal + SP-xERA + PQI + Stage agree simultaneously at pre-cap score ≥ 5) remains the only validated elevation pathway — confirmed twice on CLE@MIA (7/10 and 7/12).

Pick-column misread risk documented on 7/8 (four misreads in one session): always read the `pick` column directly from the CSV. Do NOT infer the model pick from f5_prob, full_prob, or p_model, especially when Stage 1 and Stage 2 disagree across the 50% threshold.

NYM @ PHI.reasoning: NYM correctly graded SKIP: score=0 (no F-signal fires, PQI Δ=+9.3 opposing NYM, CAP 10 fires at pick_prob=0.535). Four-dimension rescue (F-signal + SP-xERA + PQI + Stage agree at pre-cap ≥ 5) is unmet. Platoon context unavailable (vs_today_SP_OPS null throughout—lineups not yet posted). BvP: Soto is a strong OWNER vs Nola (59 PA, shrunk OPS 1.000, 0.085 HR/PA); Lindor historically struggles (42 PA, shrunk 0.666). One OWNER alone does not elevate a zero-signal pick per the BvP framework. Scott's K% edge (28.7% vs Nola 24.12%) modestly corroborates NYM's Stage 2 direction; no live injury or roster information is verifiable.
lessons_applied: CAP-10 bottom-bucket suppression correct: no F-signal fires and PQI opposes NYM — four-dimension rescue threshold unmet
BvP one-OWNER rule: Soto OWNER vs Nola (59 PA, shrunk 1.000) noted but one OWNER is insufficient to elevate a zero-signal pick
Stage 2 full-game direction (NYM 55.2%) corroborated by Scott K% edge; Stage 1 lean (PHI 60% F5) does not override Stage 2 per 7/10-7/12 high-delta pattern
Pick-column discipline: model_pick=NYM read directly from CSV `pick` column, not inferred from f5_prob or full_prob
